title: pilosella subcymigera subsp. radiata
intro: Pilosella subcymigera subsp. radiata, also known as the radiate mouse-ear hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, as the plant's sap is believed to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Pilosella subcymigera subsp. radiata include its potential to accelerate wound healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and even exhibit antiviral properties. The plant is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, with the sap being extracted and used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
